rollup.rationale:
SCMP requires server SDKs in polling mode to send two custom HTTP request headers — `X-LaunchDarkly-InstanceID` (a GUID v4 stable for the SDK instance lifetime) and `X-LaunchDarkly-PollingIntervalMs` (the configured interval) — so LaunchDarkly can estimate Server Connection Minutes for usage-based billing in polling mode. The Go server SDK currently sends neither header.

PollingRequester (FDv1) attaches only `context.GetHTTP().DefaultHeaders` (User-Agent, Authorization, etc.); the FDv2 polling requester likewise. Repo-wide search for `X-LaunchDarkly-InstanceID`, `InstanceID`, `PollingIntervalMs`, or anything resembling a per-instance GUID returns 0 matches.

All 5 SCMP requirements (1.1.1, 1.1.2, 1.2.1, 1.3.1, 1.3.2) are unimplemented. Note the bulk LLM judge in spec_support.json labeled this as `full` — that's incorrect; the deep-dive disagrees and corrects to `not_supported`. The complexity of fixing this is `trivial` (two extra headers with a one-time-generated UUID per LDClient instance). The spec is currently DRAFT, which may explain why this wasn't prioritized.

evidence_sources_considered: go-server-sdk/subsystems/changeset.go (ChangeSet type that ApplyDelta consumes — converts FDv2 deltas to []Collection)
go-server-sdk/subsystems/data_store.go (FDv1 DataStore interface — separate but interoperable surface)

rollup.rationale: TXNS is the transactional in-memory store that backs FDv2 (Flag Delivery v2): batches of updates apply atomically so concurrent reads never see a half-applied patch. The Go SDK satisfies all four requirements via `internal/memorystorev2/memory_store.go`. ApplyDelta takes a []Collection and serializes the entire batch under a single sync.RWMutex.Lock(); SetBasis discards-and-rebuilds atomically. Concurrent readers (Get/GetAll) hold an RLock and therefore either see the pre-Lock snapshot or the post-Unlock snapshot but never anything in between.
rollup.supported_since_evidence: go-server-sdk CHANGELOG: FDv2 (and the memorystorev2 transactional store backing it) landed across v7.6.0–v7.10.0; v7.10.0 (2025-01-22) is when the FDv2 data system was wired up by default-eligible. The memorystorev2 package is introduced specifically for FDv2 (per its own package doc-comment).

requirements.text: The storage interface MUST have a mechanism to support multiple updates as a single operation.
requirements.rationale: Store.ApplyDelta accepts a []ldstoretypes.Collection (a batch keyed by DataKind) and applies all upserts under a single sync.RWMutex.Lock() boundary.

findings.relevance: func (s *Store) ApplyDelta(allData []ldstoretypes.Collection) — takes a batch of collections, not single updates.
s.Lock(); defer s.Unlock() — entire batch covered by one write-lock acquisition.

requirements.text: Application of the updates MUST be atomic from the perspective of local access (readers MUST NOT see partial application).
requirements.rationale: ApplyDelta and SetBasis both acquire s.Lock() (write lock) for the duration of the entire batch. Get/GetAll acquire s.RLock() (read lock) which blocks if a write is in progress, so readers see either the pre-batch or post-batch snapshot — never a partial state.

findings.relevance: ApplyDelta: s.Lock(); defer s.Unlock() spans the entire range loop.
SetBasis: s.Lock(); defer s.Unlock() spans the discard-and-rebuild.
Get: s.RLock()/s.RUnlock() — writer's Lock() blocks readers until the batch completes.
GetAll: s.RLock(); defer s.RUnlock() — same atomicity guarantee.

requirements.text: The store interface MUST have an initialization method that accepts a list of updates.
requirements.rationale: Store.SetBasis(allData []Collection) is the initialization entry point — accepts a batch of collections (the basis) and marks the store initialized. The wider FDv1-compatible Init path lives in subsystems/ldstoreimpl for the legacy interface.

findings.relevance: func (s *Store) SetBasis(allData []ldstoretypes.Collection) — accepts a list of collections (the spec's 'list of updates').
Doc comment: 'First, the store's basis is set. This is this initial data, upon which subsequent deltas will be applied.' — matches spec phrasing exactly.

requirements.text: The initialization method MUST clear the store and apply the updates from an empty state.
requirements.rationale: SetBasis explicitly assigns s.data = make(map[...]) before populating, discarding any prior contents. Doc comment also documents this as 'Any existing data is discarded' and 'Whenever the basis is set, any existing data is discarded'.

findings.relevance: s.data = make(map[ldstoretypes.DataKind]map[string]ldstoretypes.ItemDescriptor) — fresh map replaces any prior state.
s.initialized = true — sets initialization flag only after the new basis is fully populated.
